summary: This lecture covers the fifth type of filth (najasa) according to Islamic jurisprudence as discussed in Al-Wajeez, focusing on the feces, urine, and bodily fluids of animals that are impermissible to eat. The instructor explains that while these animals and their secretions are inherently impure, there are important exceptions for domestic animals like house cats, donkeys, and hunting dogs due to practical necessity and common use in human life. The lecture also clarifies that certain animal parts like hair, teeth, and nails are not considered part of the animal (as they lack blood flow) and therefore remain pure even from impermissible animals.
suggested_title: Types of Filth (Najasa) - Animals Impermissible to Eat and Practical Exceptions in Islamic Law
hadith_chapter: Book of Purification, Chapter on Various Types of Filth
catchy_title: Which Animal Waste is Actually Impure?
